title: Metadata Predictability Is Not Evidence Dependence: An Intervention-Based Audit for Weak-Label Benchmarks
abstract: arXiv:2605.23701v2 Announce Type: replace  Abstract: We study a protocol-level test for weak-label benchmarks: whether benchmark outputs change when the provided evidence is intervened on. Metadata-only shortcut checks answer a different question, namely whether outputs are predictable from metadata priors. We therefore combine a metadata statistic, the Metadata Prior Dominance Score (MPDS), with an evidence-intervention statistic, {\Delta}Evi, measuring sensitivity to evidence identity under cross-item shuffling. Synthetic HotpotQA gives a constructed counterexample to metadata-only screening: MPDS is only moderate (0.643), yet {\Delta}Evi is zero. Stronger-reader reruns show why calibration belongs in the test procedure: SNLI shows a calibration reversal, reconstructed HotpotQA occupies a question-dominant warning region, and FEVER is a strongly evidence-sensitive positive control across four transformers. The practical lesson is simple: benchmark audits should report metadata-only s

Title: Metadata Predictability Is Not Evidence Dependence: An Intervention-Based Audit for Weak-Label Benchmarks
Abstract: arXiv:2605.23701v2 Announce Type: replace  Abstract: We study a protocol-level test for weak-label benchmarks: whether benchmark outputs change when the provided evidence is intervened on. Metadata-only shortcut checks answer a different question, namely whether outputs are predictable from metadata priors. Synthetic HotpotQA gives a constructed counterexample to metadata-only screening: MPDS is only moderate (0.643), yet {\Delta}Evi is zero. Stronger-reader reruns show why calibration belongs in the test procedure: SNLI shows a calibration reversal, reconstructed HotpotQA occupies a question-dominant warning region, and FEVER is a strongly evidence-sensitive positive control across four transformers. translated_title: 元数据可预测性并非证据依赖：针对弱标签基准的干预式审计
translated_abstract: 我们研究了一种针对弱标签基准的协议级测试：当提供的证据被干预时，基准输出是否发生变化。仅基于元数据的捷径检查回答的是另一个问题，即输出是否可以从元数据先验中预测出来。因此，我们将元数据统计量——元数据先验主导分数（MPDS）与证据干预统计量ΔEvi相结合，后者通过跨项目洗牌测量对证据身份的敏感性。合成HotpotQA为纯元数据筛选提供了一个构造的反例：MPDS仅为中等水平（0.643），而ΔEvi为零。更强的阅读器重新运行显示了校准为何应纳入测试流程：SNLI展示了校准反转，重建的HotpotQA占据了一个问题主导的警告区域，而FEVER在四个变换器上是一个强证据敏感的正向对照。实际教训很简单：基准审计应报告纯元数据筛查和证据干预测试的结果。
tldr: 本文提出结合元数据统计（MPDS）与证据干预统计（ΔEvi）的审计方法，通过合成反例和实际基准测试证明，仅依赖元数据可预测性不足以判断基准对证据的依赖，必须同时进行证据干预测试。
en_tdlr: This paper proposes an audit method combining metadata statistics (MPDS) with evidence-intervention statistics (ΔEvi), demonstrating through synthetic counterexamples and real benchmarks that metadata-only predictability is insufficient to judge evidence dependence, necessitating concurrent evidence-intervention tests.
